senses.definition: a fish that makes a whistling or singing sound, often using its swim bladder
examples: The singingfish emitted a melodic hum as dusk fell.
Scientists studied the singingfish to understand how it produces sound.

senses.definition: plural of singingfish; more than one singingfish
examples: Researchers collected several singingfishes for the experiment.
The tank contained singingfishes from different habitats.

senses.definition: in a singing manner; in a way that imitates singing or producing musical tones
examples: The birds chirped singingly as the sun rose.
She spoke singingly, as if singing a soft tune.

senses.definition: the act or instance of singing; songs collectively
examples: Her singings were greeted with applause.
They enjoyed the evening of traditional singings.

senses.definition: a tropical tuber root vegetable also known as yautía or cassava keps; often used in Caribbean and Filipino cuisines
examples: The dish features boiled singkamas with a garlic oil drizzle.
Singkamas is grated into salads in some Southeast Asian recipes.

senses.definition: not shared with others; one and only
examples: She lives in a small single room.
He bought a single ticket to London.

senses.definition: one person or thing that is not paired or grouped with others
examples: Only a single remained after the flood.
She kept a single in her pocket for luck.

senses.definition: to cause to be or become single; to connect two or more items into one
examples: The two pieces were singling together to form a new table.
They singlized the data set to remove duplicates.

senses.definition: a bar designed to be used singly or a stand-alone bar in a structure; or a bar with a single unit
examples: The frame uses a singlebar for the upright support.

senses.definition: past tense of single; to set apart or select as single; to place alone or apart
examples: She singled him out from the group for the award.
The experiment singled the variable for closer study.

senses.definition: the state of being single; single life
examples: He preferred singledom to marriage at that time.
The book explores the challenges of singledom in modern society.

senses.definition: plural of singledom; multiple states of being single
examples: The conference discussed various singledoms across cultures.
Different singledoms have different social expectations.

senses.definition: in a very focused and determined way; with a single aim
examples: She worked singlemindedly to complete the project.
He pursued the goal singlemindedly, ignoring distractions.

senses.definition: the state of not being married or involved in a romantic relationship; the quality of being single
examples: His singleness surprised some friends.
She valued singleness during her early career.

senses.definition: plural of singleness; multiple instances of being single or the quality of being single
examples: The report notes several singlenesses in social structures.
Various singlenesses affect dating norms.

senses.definition: a representation of a number using a single precision in computing, typically 32 bits
examples: The graphics engine uses singleprecision floating-point numbers.
For memory efficiency, use singleprecision where possible.

senses.definition: a person or thing that singles; a single person or item
examples: He is a fast singer and a singular singler in the team.
The app highlights the singler among duplicates.

senses.definition: records or songs released individually, not as part of an album; people who are not in a romantic relationship
examples: She released several hit singles.
There were two singles at the party who were new to the area.

senses.definition: plural of single; people who are not in a relationship
examples: The event was popular among singles.
Singles often have different social circles than couples.

senses.definition: a single step in a process or movement
examples: Follow the instructions one singlestep at a time.
The algorithm performs a singlestep update.
